cards.back: A sequential search algorithm checks each item in a list or array one by one until it finds a match or reaches the end.

cards.front: What is Brute Force String Matching?
cards.back: A brute force string matching algorithm checks every possible substring of the text and compares it with the pattern to find a match.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Sequential Search?
cards.back: The time complexity of sequential search is O(n), where n is the number of elements in the list or array.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Brute Force String Matching?
cards.back: The time complexity of brute force string matching is O(n*m), where n is the length of the text and m is the length of the pattern.

cards.front: What is the main difference between Sequential Search and Brute Force String Matching?
cards.back: The main difference is that sequential search searches for a single item in a list, while brute force string matching searches for all possible substrings of a text to find a match.
